task_completion.description: Agent opens or loads the source blood pressure data file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent opening the blood pressure dataset in a spreadsheet application, text editor, or loading it via a script.

task_completion.description: Agent identifies and handles missing data entries
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent filtering out, skipping, or explicitly handling the rows that have missing systolic/diastolic values.

task_completion.description: Agent calculates average readings by time of day
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent calculating the average systolic and diastolic readings grouped by morning and evening. Look for values around 126.2/80.2 for morning and 138.4/88.9 for evening.

task_completion.description: Agent identifies and flags readings that exceed medical thresholds
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent applying medical thresholds (e.g., Elevated, Stage 1, Stage 2) to categorize the readings and flag the concerning ones.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the analysis and flagged readings to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent saving or writing the final compiled report to exactly `~/Documents/blood_pressure_report.csv`.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely calculates the analysis rather than using hardcoded answers
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of actual computation, such as spreadsheet formulas, Python/bash scripts, or data analysis tool usage, rather than the agent just typing out the final expected numbers.

integrity.description: Agent uses the provided dataset without hallucinating data
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's analysis is based on the actual rows in the provided file (36 valid readings, 2 missing) and does not invent new readings or dates.

integrity.description: Agent saves the output in the correct format and location
integrity.visual_evidence: The final file is saved exactly at `~/Documents/blood_pressure_report.csv` and is formatted as a CSV, not a plain text document or saved in a different directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 38 rows with 36 valid readings and 2 missing entries. Morning averages (19 readings, before 12:00) are 126.21 systolic / 80.21 diastolic. Evening averages (17 readings, after 18:00) are 138.35 systolic / 88.94 diastolic. Medical thresholds align with ACC/AHA guidelines: Normal (<120/<80), Elevated (120-129/<80), Stage 1 (130-139 or 80-89), Stage 2 (>=140 or >=90). The correct categorization of the 36 valid readings is: 1 Normal, 6 Elevated, 22 Stage 1, 7 Stage 2, and 0 Crisis.
